name: garage
description: A Pulumi provider to manage Garage S3-compatible object storage.

adminToken.description: A bearer token authorized against the Garage Admin API. Falls back to the GARAGE_ADMIN_TOKEN environment variable if not set.

endpoint.description: The base URL of the Garage Admin API, e.g. "http://localhost:3903". Falls back to the GARAGE_ADMIN_ENDPOINT environment variable if not set.

owner.description: Whether the key has owner rights on the bucket (manage bucket-level settings such as its website configuration or quotas via the S3 API).

read.description: Whether the key can read objects (GetObject, ListObjects, ...) from the bucket.

write.description: Whether the key can write objects (PutObject, DeleteObject, ...) to the bucket.

maxObjects.description: The maximum number of objects the bucket may hold. Unset means no limit.

maxSize.description: The maximum total size, in bytes, the bucket may hold. Unset means no limit.

errorDocument.description: The document served for requests that don't match an existing object. Defaults to Garage's built-in error page if unset.

indexDocument.description: The document served for requests to the bucket root or any "directory".

adminToken.description: A bearer token authorized against the Garage Admin API. Falls back to the GARAGE_ADMIN_TOKEN environment variable if not set.

endpoint.description: The base URL of the Garage Admin API, e.g. "http://localhost:3903". Falls back to the GARAGE_ADMIN_ENDPOINT environment variable if not set.

adminToken.description: A bearer token authorized against the Garage Admin API. Falls back to the GARAGE_ADMIN_TOKEN environment variable if not set.

endpoint.description: The base URL of the Garage Admin API, e.g. "http://localhost:3903". Falls back to the GARAGE_ADMIN_ENDPOINT environment variable if not set.

bytes.description: The total size, in bytes, of all objects currently stored in the bucket.

createdAt.description: The RFC 3339 timestamp at which the bucket was created.

globalAlias.description: The bucket's human-readable global alias, e.g. "my-app-data". Buckets can be created without one and addressed only by ID, but an alias is required to use the bucket over the S3 API with most clients. Only a single global alias is supported; local (per-key) aliases and multiple global aliases are not managed by this provider.

objects.description: The number of objects currently stored in the bucket.

quotas.description: Storage quotas for the bucket. Omit either field, or the whole block, to leave that limit unset.

website.description: Static-website hosting configuration for the bucket. Omit to leave website hosting disabled.

globalAlias.description: The bucket's human-readable global alias, e.g. "my-app-data". Buckets can be created without one and addressed only by ID, but an alias is required to use the bucket over the S3 API with most clients. Only a single global alias is supported; local (per-key) aliases and multiple global aliases are not managed by this provider.

quotas.description: Storage quotas for the bucket. Omit either field, or the whole block, to leave that limit unset.

website.description: Static-website hosting configuration for the bucket. Omit to leave website hosting disabled.

accessKeyId.description: The access key ID of the Key to grant permissions to.

bucketId.description: The ID of the Bucket to grant permissions on.

accessKeyId.description: The access key ID of the Key to grant permissions to.

bucketId.description: The ID of the Bucket to grant permissions on.

accessKeyId.description: The S3 access key ID, e.g. the value of AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID.

createdAt.description: The RFC 3339 timestamp at which the key was created.

name.description: A human-readable label for the key. If unset, Garage assigns a default name.

secretAccessKey.description: The S3 secret access key, e.g. the value of AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY. Only ever readable at creation time - Garage's Admin API does not return it again afterwards, so it is captured once here and carried forward in state.

name.description: A human-readable label for the key. If unset, Garage assigns a default name.
